judgment.summary: A large, profitable healthcare provider with solid analyst backing, but facing modest near-term growth and significant debt.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat EPS estimates by 9.5%; next earnings date is 2026-07-28.
positives: Forward P/E of 18.42x is attractive relative to trailing P/E of 28.84x, indicating expected earnings growth.
Strong free cash flow of $17.69B TTM supports operations and dividends.
Analyst consensus is a strong buy with a mean target price suggesting upside.
Dividend yield of 2.3% offers income to shareholders.
negatives: Revenue growth of 2.0% YoY and earnings growth of 0.7% YoY are very modest.
Total debt of $77.92B significantly outweighs total cash of $31.23B, leading to a high Debt/Equity ratio of 73.98%.
Current ratio of 0.80 and quick ratio of 0.72 suggest potential short-term liquidity concerns.

news.paragraph: UnitedHealth Group is seeing positive momentum as its managed care segment outpaces the broader economy. This news suggests underlying strength in a key business line, potentially driving future growth. Traders should consider this positive operational trend ahead of the upcoming July earnings report.

headlines.title: UnitedHealth Group (UNH) Gains as Managed Care Outgrows Economy
